calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE.

No appearances made in this matter. The court adopts its tentative ruling.

Pinnacle Brands LLC has submitted a declaration in response to the court's order to show cause stating that it has not sold the product in California since 2021 and that it lacks the funds to pay all of the attorney's fees it owes to plaintiff Precila Balabbo at this time. The court discharges the order to show cause. Pinnacle has shown its compliance with the nonmonetary provisions of the judgment. Balabbo has a judgment for money that may be enforced using the regular avenues for collection of a judgment. The settlement agreement does not separately contain a provision for sanctions in the event of a breach of the agreement.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Consent Judgment And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF PRECILA BALABBO's Motion To Enforce Consent Judgment And For Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion to enforce consent judgment and for sanctions is granted in part. Plaintiff shows that defendant Pinnacle Brands LLC has not complied with its payment obligations under the settlement agreement. The court orders Pinnacle Brands to make payment of all outstanding amounts to Plaintiff within 15 days of entry of this order and to provide a verified statement to Plaintiffs and the court that sales of the products at issue in this case comply with the warning requirements of Proposition 65 as described in the Consent Judgment. The statement shall be provided within 15 days of entry of this order.

The court issues an order to show cause and sets the matter for hearing on July 18, 2025. By that date in open court, or by verified statement submitted at least five court days in advance of hearing, Pinnacle Brands shall show cause why it should not be sanctioned in the amount of $3000, payable to Plaintiff, for failure to make payment of outstanding amounts under the parties' settlement agreement and for failure to comply with the warning requirements of Proposition 65 with respect to the hand sanitizer products that are the subject of Plaintiff's action, as required by this order and the consent judgment.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-08-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF PRECILA BALABBO's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's unopposed motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment is granted.

Order signed. Consent Judgment signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
